strengths: 15+ years of fullstack experience spanning TypeScript, Node.js, React, and multiple paradigms — far exceeds the 6-year minimum, with senior/staff titles across five consecutive roles
Current role at re:cinq includes explicit hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work (MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks) — a direct match to the AI agent development requirement

Demonstrated cloud and containerization experience: AWS CDK at Optilyz, Kubernetes deployment for GISCollective, Docker at Altom; MongoDB at production scale (millions of mails at Optilyz, millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
concerns: NestJS does not appear anywhere in the resume despite being the primary backend framework in the role's stack — represents a concrete ramp-up cost for the team's core technology
Kotlin is entirely absent from skills and employment history; Java surfaces only in older embedded and hardware contexts (robotic arms, NI LabWindows), not backend services

Candidate name 'Aisha Okonkwo' does not match any contact detail on the resume — email, website, GitHub, and LinkedIn all resolve to 'Bogdan Szabo,' which is an unresolved identity-verification concern that must be clarified before advancing
response.justification: The candidate has a strong, well-evidenced fullstack background in TypeScript and Node.js, and current work on MCP-based AI-agent tooling is a direct hit on one of the role's less common requirements. Cloud, containerization, and MongoDB coverage are solid. The primary technical gap is NestJS — the role's core backend framework — which is absent from the resume entirely; an experienced Node.js engineer can learn it, but it is a real onboarding cost. Kotlin is also missing, though Java appears in peripheral hardware contexts. More immediately, the name/contact-info mismatch (header reads 'Aisha Okonkwo' but every identifier points to 'Bogdan Szabo') must be resolved before an interview is scheduled, as it may indicate a template substitution error, a proxy submission, or identity misrepresentation. Score reflects strong technical evidence discounted for the NestJS gap and the unresolved identity issue.
